notes: Story hook: owner discovers during MOL audit that employment contracts are missing/unsigned. Real cost beyond the fine.
title: Employee contracts stored in a drawer — why that matters far more than most owners realise
brief: Story hook: owner discovers during MOL audit that employment contracts are missing/unsigned. Real cost beyond the fine.
body:
An MOL inspector walked into a 38-person company in Muscat and asked for one thing.

Employment contracts. All of them. In Arabic. Signed. Filed.

The owner had been running his business for six years. He had contracts — somewhere. Printed, mostly. Some scanned. A few were still unsigned because the employee had "meant to come back and sort it."

He spent the next four hours on the phone with his HR consultant, his lawyer, and his bank.

The fine wasn't the expensive part. The four hours were.

Because those four hours were the CEO's hours — the Monday morning he should have spent closing two deals he'd been building for three months. One of those prospects called while he was on the phone with the inspector. He couldn't pick up. The prospect moved to a competitor.

The inspector comes back in 30 days.

Here's the thing most owners don't realise: employment contracts aren't a legal formality. They're the baseline of every other HR process you run — end-of-service calculations, WPS submissions, Ministry of Manpower filings, visa renewals. If the contract isn't right, everything downstream is wrong.

And "stored in a drawer" is not an audit trail.

SmartPRO Hub keeps every employment contract digitally signed, archived, and searchable. When the inspector asks — you click, not scramble.

When was the last time you could put your hand on every signed contract in under two minutes?
